title: AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md Templates
hook: A real AGENTS.md with real opinions. Not the generic template.
description: Working templates for CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md — project-specific, opinionated, short enough to actually be loaded every prompt. Comes with two worked examples: a Next.js monorepo and a Python FastAPI service.
previewContent:
```md
# RuleSell

## Stack
- Next.js 16 App Router | pnpm | Vitest | ESLint
- Prisma 7 + Postgres | NextAuth v5
- Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui

## Commands
- Dev: `npm run dev` (port 3777)
- Test single: `npx vitest run <path>`
- Build: `npm run build`
```

Plus don't-touch lists, env expectations, domain glossary.
body:
# AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md Templates

Both files do the same job: tell the agent what's specific about this project that it can't learn from the code. Different ecosystems prefer different filenames — CLAUDE.md for Claude Code, AGENTS.md for Codex and most other tools.

**The rule**: keep it under 200 lines, say things only this project knows, no generic best practices.

## Template 1 — Full-stack Next.js / Prisma / Postgres / TypeScript

```md
# {{project name}}

{{one sentence on what this is}}

## Stack

- **Runtime**: Node.js 24 LTS | pnpm | Turbopack
- **Framework**: Next.js 16.x App Router, React 19
- **Types**: TypeScript 5.x strict
- **DB**: Prisma 7 + PostgreSQL (pooled via PgBouncer in prod)
- **Auth**: NextAuth v5 (Authjs), credentials + GitHub + Google
- **Styling**: Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui
- **i18n**: next-intl (EN + TR)
- **Payments**: LemonSqueezy (MoR) + Stripe Connect (future, for payouts)
- **Storage**: Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible) for uploads
- **Rate limit**: Upstash Redis via @upstash/ratelimit
- **Deploy**: Vercel (frontend) + Railway (Postgres)

## Commands

```bash
# Dev
npm run dev                  # localhost:3000

# Test
npm test                     # all tests
npx vitest run <path>        # single file
npm run test:api             # API route tests only

# DB
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>
npx prisma generate
npx prisma studio
npm run db:seed              # seed script at prisma/seed.ts

# Build
npm run build                # prisma generate && next build

# Deploy
vercel --prod                # from linked project
```

## Don't touch

- `src/generated/prisma/` — auto-generated, never hand-edit
- `.next/` — build output
- `prisma/migrations/` — only via `prisma migrate dev`
- `messages/*.json` — edit through the i18n helper, not by hand (keeps TR in sync with EN structure)
- `railway.toml`, `vercel.json` — changing build config is a deploy event

## Conventions

- Server Components by default; `'use client'` only when you need state, effects, or event handlers
- Data access only via Prisma `db` client at `@/lib/db` — never `new PrismaClient()` in feature code
- API routes live at `src/app/api/*/route.ts`
- Client-side data fetching uses SWR — `useRulesets`, `useCollections` hooks
- Auth check via NextAuth's `auth()` helper on the server, `useSession()` on the client
- Feature components live under `src/components/<feature>/*`
- Shared primitives live under `src/components/ui/` (shadcn)

## Environment variables

- Local: `.env.local` (never committed)
- CI/Prod: Vercel env vars (sync via `vercel env pull`)
- Required: `DATABASE_URL`, `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`, `NEXTAUTH_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`
- OAuth: `GITHUB_CLIENT_ID/SECRET`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET`
- Optional (graceful fallback if missing): `RESEND_API_KEY`, `UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_*`, R2 keys, LEMONSQUEEZY_*

When a variable is missing in production, the app should log the absence once (not on every request) and fall back to a safe default.

## Domain glossary

- **Ruleset** — a single listing on the marketplace (any type)
- **Variant** — a sub-listing of a ruleset, specific to an environment
- **Platform** — Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.
- **Quality Score** — 0-100, composed of 6 signals
- **Trader KYC** — DSA Art 30 identity verification
- **MoR** — Merchant of Record (the legal seller for tax purposes)
- **Certified Dev** — rung 4 creator with reviewer privileges

## What to look at first

- `prisma/schema.prisma` — the data model
- `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` — root layout with metadata
- `src/lib/db.ts` — Prisma client
- `src/lib/auth.ts` — NextAuth config
- `src/proxy.ts` — middleware (locale, auth, compliance)
- `docs/frontend-api-contract.md` — every API endpoint the frontend calls

## Deploy

- Vercel (primary): push to `master` → auto-deploy
- Railway (fallback): also on `master`, separate build
- Production URL: https://rulesell.vercel.app
```

## Template 2 — Python FastAPI service

```md
# {{service name}}

## Stack

- Python 3.13 | uv | ruff | pytest
- FastAPI | Pydantic v2 | SQLModel
- PostgreSQL via asyncpg
- Deployed to Vercel via Fluid Compute

## Commands

```bash
uv run uvicorn app:app --reload              # dev server
uv run pytest                                  # tests
uv run pytest tests/api -k endpoint_name      # single test
uv run ruff check .                           # lint
uv run ruff format .                          # format
uv run alembic upgrade head                   # migrate
uv run alembic revision -m 'description' --autogenerate  # new migration
```

## Don't touch

- `alembic/versions/` unless creating a new migration
- `.venv/` — ignored
- `__pycache__/` — ignored

## Conventions

- Every endpoint has a Pydantic response model
- Every database query goes through a repository function, not inline in the route
- Errors use FastAPI's HTTPException with a structured detail dict
- Tests use a separate `test_` database with transaction rollback

## Environment variables

- `DATABASE_URL` — asyncpg connection string
- `SECRET_KEY` — for JWT
- `SENTRY_DSN` — optional
```

## How to write a good project instructions file

1. **Keep it < 200 lines.** Use `@` imports to pull in deeper material on demand.
2. **Every fact must be specific to this project.** "Use TDD" is not specific. "Tests run via `pnpm test --filter web` and cover the `apps/web` package" is specific.
3. **Update when commands change.** Stale commands are worse than no commands.
4. **Include the Don't Touch list.** Saves hours of agent confusion.
5. **Include a Domain glossary** for any project with non-trivial vocabulary.

## License

MIT. Copy and adapt.

---

*Tested against Claude Code and Codex CLI as of 2026-04.*
